title: amoora ganggo
intro: Amoora ganggo, also known as the bastard teak, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Amoora ganggo include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and astringent. Herbal preparations of Amoora ganggo include the bark and leaves being made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions.
